channel.title: Inference — AI Newsletter
channel.description: The signal in the noise. Curated AI trends, tools, and research — updated every Monday.

item.title: Will the Pentagon’s Anthropic controversy scare startups away from defense work?
item.description: A TechCrunch podcast discussed whether the Pentagon's issues with Anthropic will deter startups from defense contracts. The podcast speculates that increased scrutiny and potential political fallout may make defense work less attractive despite the lucrative opportunities.
item.author: Anthony Ha

item.title: Google BigQuery Previews Cross-Region SQL Queries for Distributed Data
item.description: Google BigQuery's new global queries feature, now in preview, allows SQL queries across different geographic regions without needing to move or copy data first. This simplifies data aggregation for distributed datasets.
item.author: Renato Losio

item.title: Google's new command-line tool can plug OpenClaw into your Workspace data
item.description: Google has released OpenClaw, a command-line tool for interacting with Google Workspace data. While not an official product, OpenClaw simplifies the use of multiple Workspace APIs from the command line.
item.author: Ryan Whitwam

item.title: LWiAI Podcast #235 - Sonnet 4.6, Deep-thinking tokens, Anthropic vs Pentagon
item.description: Anthropic released Sonnet 4.6, a faster and cheaper model than Claude 3 Opus, while Google launched Gemini 1.5 Pro. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei stated that threats from the Pentagon won't change their AI stance.
item.author: Last Week in AI

item.title: How Balyasny Asset Management built an AI research engine for investing
item.description: Balyasny Asset Management created an AI research engine using GPT models and agent workflows to improve investment analysis. Their system uses rigorous model evaluation to ensure quality and reliability in financial decision-making.
item.author: OpenAI Blog

item.title: AutoGPT
item.description: Auto-GPT is an open-source Python application that uses the GPT-4 language model to autonomously achieve any goal you set. With 182k stars and 46k forks, it's a popular starting point for building autonomous agents.
item.author: Significant-Gravitas

item.title: system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools
item.description: A GitHub repo aggregates system prompts, internal tools, and AI models used in 28 different AI coding tools, including Cursor and Replit. The collection has amassed nearly 130k stars and 33k forks.
item.author: x1xhlol

item.title: openclaw
item.description: OpenClaw is a self-hosted personal AI assistant that connects to 20+ messaging platforms via a local Gateway control plane, with companion apps for macOS, iOS, and Android.
item.author: openclaw

item.title: Write C Code Without Learning C: The Magic of PythoC
item.description: Write update worth a look if it overlaps with your current work (via Towards Data Science).
item.author: Thomas Reid

item.title: Grammarly is using our identities without permission
item.description: Grammarly's expert review feature uses writing advice inspired by subject matter experts; one user found their boss listed as an expert without prior consent. The AI-generated feedback raises questions about the sourcing and permissions around the expert advice.
item.author: Stevie Bonifield

item.title: Beyond Accuracy: Quantifying the Production Fragility Caused by Excessive, Redundant, and Low-Signal Features in Regression
item.description: A new study quantifies how adding features to regression models can increase production fragility, even if accuracy improves. The research introduces a metric, fragility score, to measure the risk associated with each feature based on its redundancy and signal strength. High fragility scores indicate features.
item.author: Arham Islam

item.title: Introducing Modular Diffusers - Composable Building Blocks for Diffusion Pipelines
item.description: Hugging Face released Modular Diffusers, letting users build diffusion pipelines from reusable components instead of writing them from scratch. The library complements the existing `DiffusionPipeline` class, offering a more flexible and composable approach.
item.author: Hugging Face Blog

item.title: This Jammer Wants to Block Always-Listening AI Wearables. It Probably Won’t Work
item.description: Deveillances creator acknowledges the difficulty of jamming effectively across diverse environments and devices.
item.author: Boone Ashworth

item.title: I Built an Agent Skill That Generates Full Videos From a Topic Name
item.description: An AI agent skill can now generate full explainer videos from a topic name using tools like Remotion, Flux, and Chatterbox TTS. The agent automates research, script writing, image generation, scene rendering, and voiceover, creating a 2-minute video with 18 scenes from a single prompt.
item.author: William Donovan

item.title: How our open-source AI model SpeciesNet is helping to promote wildlife conservation
item.description: Google's open-source SpeciesNet AI model helps conservationists analyse motion-triggered camera images of wildlife. SpeciesNet reduces the time needed to turn snapshots into actionable data, which is normally a very time-consuming task.
item.author: Tanya Birch, Dan Morris

item.title: Online harassment is entering its AI era
item.description: MIT Technology Review reports on how generative AI is being weaponised for online harassment — deepfakes, synthetic voice cloning, and AI-generated abuse at scale are outpacing platform moderation.
item.author: Grace Huckins
